app_error_body: Something went wrong. Please try again.
app_error_body_contactSupport: If you contact us regarding the error, please mention the following trace ID {traceId}
app_error_business_BUSINESS_DATA_INTEGRI: The operation conflicts with existing business data.
app_error_business_DATA_INVALID_VIRUS_DE: The uploaded file was blocked because it contains a virus.

app_error_business_IDENTIFIER_VALIDATION: The identifier format or value is invalid.
app_error_business_INVALID_PAGINATION_me: The requested pagination parameters are invalid.
app_error_business_MAX_SIZE_EXCEEDED_mes: The uploaded content exceeds the maximum allowed size.
app_error_business_OBJECT_COUNT_LIMIT_RE: The maximum number of allowed objects has been reached.
app_error_business_RESOURCE_FORBIDDEN_me: You do not have permission to access this resource.
app_error_business_RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND_me: The requested resource could not be found.
app_error_default_message: Something went wrong. Please try again.
app_error_http_400_message: The request could not be processed.
app_error_http_403_message: You are not allowed to perform this action.
app_error_http_404_message: The requested resource was not found.
app_error_http_502_message: Received an invalid response from an upstream service.
app_serviceNav_help_additionalInformatio: Further information on swiyu Public Beta
app_serviceNav_help_github_discussions: Ask your questions and exchange ideas with the community via GitHub discussions
app_site_onboarding_trust_introduction_e: Profile verification for the trust register
app_site_overview_empty_body_p1: Get enrolled as a Business Partner in the swiyu beta Trust Infrastructure to onboard the beta Base Registry and issue demo credentials
app_site_overview_empty_body_p2: For this you will need: a valid email address
app_site_overview_notification_createOrg: Your organisation is now officially registered as a Business Patner on swiyu Trust Infrastructure.
You now have access to the APIs in order to onboard the beta Base Registry.
app_site_overview_notification_organizat: Not more than {max} organizations can be created. Please conteact onboardingBeta@swiyu.admin.ch to get unused organizations deleted
app_site_overview_notification_registerS: Visit the API self-service portal to subscribe to the two newly granted APIs and get the access tokens that will allow you to onboard the beta Base Registry.
Important: Make sure to copy and securely store both access tokens
app_site_overview_notification_registerT: Enroll in the beta Trust Registry
Authenticate your newly registered organisation to act as a verified actor in the beta Trust Infrastructure.
Complete a simple email form with a few basic details.
app_site_overview_notification_updateOrg: Your organization information has been updated
app_site_overview_subtitle: Organisations registered as Business Partner and assigned to your account
app_site_overview_tile_baseRegistry_body: Onboard the beta Trust Infrastructure to issue and verify demo credentials
app_site_overview_tile_baseRegistry_coll: Find all detailed step by step instructions on github
Register your organisation (individuals allowed) as a Business Partner on swiyu Trust Infrastructure
Go to the API self-service portal and subscribe to the two granted APIs
Copy and store locally the displayed access tokens
Authenticate in the API to create your DID and onboard to the beta Base Registry
app_site_overview_tile_trustRegistry_bod: Authenticate your organisation to act as a verified actor in the beta Trust Infrastructure
app_site_overview_tile_trustRegistry_col: Click on button above and fill-up the requested information in the email template
Send the completed email form to the pre-set address
You will receive the registration confirmation by email after your application has been checked. Your organisation will now be displayed to users with the ‘Verified’ badge.
app_site_overview_tile_trustRegistry_reg: Enroll in the beta Trust Registry
app_site_register_body: Provide the following details to receive the access tokens to onboard the beta Base Registry of the swiyu Public Beta Trust Infrastructure.
app_site_register_btn_conditionsCheck: I have read and agreed to the terms of use and the privacy policy
app_site_register_form_entityName: Organisation Name (company, institution or natural person)
app_site_register_hint_conditionsCheck: Please read and accept the terms of use and privacy policy for the swiyu Public Beta Trust Infrastructure if you would like to proceed with onboarding.
app_site_register_link_privacyPolicy_hin: Privacy policy swiyu Public Beta Trust Infrastructure
app_site_register_link_tos_hint: Terms of use swiyu Public Beta Trust Infrastructure
eportal_GOV_profile_access_cardText: Your institution's authorization will be verified. You will be contacted by email or phone for identification. After successful verification, your ePortal access will be activated and you will receive a confirmation by email.
eportal_GOV_profile_setup_cardText: Log in again with your ePortal account and open the swiyu Service Portal. You can now create a profile for your authority and use the service free of charge.
eportal_GOV_profile_submit_cardText: Government authorities such as cantons, municipalities or federal offices can apply for free and simplified access to the trust registry. To proceed as a government authority, activation is required. Open the email template using the "Contact us and submit request" button and enter the required information. If multiple people should have access to the authority profile, please add their names and email addresses.
eportal_GOV_start_tooltip_text_p1: To continue as a government agency or administrative body, please request access by clicking the “Request Access for Government Agencies” button and fill in the required information.
eportal_GOV_start_tooltip_text_p2: If multiple people are to be granted access, please provide their names and email addresses.
eportal_GOV_start_tooltip_text_p3: After verification and identification via email or phone, your ePortal account will be activated, and you will receive a confirmation email.
eportal_adminOverview_action_enterIdenti: Some DIDs cannot yet interact with the swiyu Wallet.
eportal_adminOverview_action_partnerProf: Manage your identity, address and contact details.
eportal_adminOverview_action_renewVerifi: Your profile verification needs to be renewed.
eportal_adminOverview_action_setupDID_te: Set up a new identifier for your wallet.
eportal_adminOverview_action_supplementR: Please supplement or correct your profile verification application and resubmit it.
eportal_adminOverview_action_verifyProfi: Have your identity verified to interact with the swiyu Wallet.
eportal_adminOverview_alertContinue: All your proof of ownership has been successfully registered. Now submit your profile verification request.
eportal_adminOverview_alertDIDsetup: No DIDs have been set up for this profile yet. Click on “Set up DID” for further instructions.
eportal_adminOverview_alertSuccess: Your swiyu profile has been successfully entered in the trust register. The verification is valid for 5 years.
eportal_adminOverview_alert_informationR: Your profile verification request requires revision.
eportal_adminOverview_didSpaces_tooltip_: Do you need DIDs for additional digital services? Purchase additional DID space here.\nYou can create up to 100 DIDs for a swiyu profile.
eportal_adminOverview_informationRequest: During the review of your application, it was found that the information does not yet meet the official requirements. Open the process to review and adjust the information.
eportal_adminOverview_notification_organ: No more than {maxBusinessPartnerPerCustomer} business partners can be created. Please contact us at onboardingBeta@swiyu.admin.ch to delete unused profiles.
eportal_adminOverview_upgradeTrustLevel_: When users interact with your digital services, they will see that you have an unverified profile. Start the verification process and have your identity officially verified so that users will see you as a verified account.
eportal_global_adminDID_error_labelChoos: Please select at least one DID by clicking one of the checkboxes
eportal_global_error_confirm: Please confirm the correctness of your details
eportal_global_inputLabel_error_correspL: Please select your preferred language for correspondence
eportal_global_inputLabel_error_emailFor: Please use the correct format: name@domain.ch
eportal_global_inputLabel_error_name: Please enter first and last name
eportal_global_inputLabel_error_phone: Please enter phone number including country code
eportal_global_inputLabel_error_phone_fo: Please use the format +41 xx xxx xx xx or 0041 xx xxx xx xx
Please use the international country code + or 00

eportal_global_unsavedChanges_body: The data update will be cancelled. All changes will be discarded.
eportal_global_uploadArea_error_addition: Please upload at least one official document
eportal_global_uploadArea_error_electron: Please upload the digitally signed declaration of intent
eportal_onboardingTR_addDetails_error_te: Please accept the terms and conditions
eportal_onboardingTR_addDetails_introTex: For official verification, the information in your swiyu profile must match the commercial register entry, the regulations or the statutes. Please check your details and complete or correct them if necessary.
Note: A regular application is subject to official review. In the test environment, your application is only checked automatically.
eportal_onboardingTR_addDetails_privacyP: I have read the data protection regulations and agree to them *
eportal_onboardingTR_addDetails_sectionI: List all persons who will sign your application. They must be authorized to do so according to the commercial register entry or signature regulations.
eportal_onboardingTR_addDetails_termsAnd: I have read the terms and conditions and agree to them *
eportal_onboardingTR_awaitVerification_s: Thank you for your application for entry in the Trust Registry. The application is now being reviewed.
The review usually takes five working days. Upon completion, you will be informed by e-mail and in the administration overview. You will also be notified if there are any queries.
eportal_onboardingTR_development_emailTe:
Hello

You are invited to perform a technical proof of possession for the swiyu Trust Registry.


-- Sign in to ePortal: Use an eIAM-compatible login to sign in to ePortal.

ePortal: https://eportal.admin.ch/


-- Access authorization: If you do not have access to the business partner, you need an invitation code. This code can be generated by the person sending this e-mail.

Generate code: https://swiyu-admin-ch.github.io/cookbooks/onboarding-base-and-trust-registry/#5-user-management

Redeem code: https://eportal.admin.ch/redeem


-- Perform proof of possession: Follow the instructions in the swiyu Service Portal. Prompts and additional technical documentation are available.

Direct link: {handoverUrl}

swiyu Service Portal: {servicePortalUrl}

Open manually: Navigate to the swiyu Service Portal. Open the organization or business partner from the list. Click "Continue profile verification" on the following page to display the technical verification instructions.


Thank you and kind regards
Handover of technical proof of possession for the swiyu Trust Registry
eportal_onboardingTR_formalProof_declara: The signatures do not meet the requirements for a QES. Please ensure that all signatures are QES.
The validation service is currently unavailable. Please try again later.
The document must contain exactly three signatures for joint signature by three.
The document must contain exactly two signatures for joint signature by two.
The document must contain exactly one signature for single signature.
eportal_onboardingTR_formalProof_introTe: For entry in the trust register, proof must be provided that the profile and the associated Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) belong unequivocally to the applicant. For this purpose, a declaration (declaration of intent) is generated, which must be signed with a qualified electronic signature (QES).
Note: A regular application is subject to official review. In the test environment, your application is only checked automatically.
eportal_onboardingTR_formalProof_section: As your organization is not entered in the commercial register, alternative proof must be submitted. Please upload your articles of association or another official document that shows the signing authorizations.
Download the document and check that all information is complete and correct.

Have the declaration of intent digitally signed by the authorized signatories of your organization. Only qualified electronic signatures (QES) – the legally recognized form of digital signature in Switzerland – are permitted. Then upload the document here.
Note: In the test environment, the declaration of intent can be uploaded without a signature.
eportal_onboardingTR_overview_cardText_a: Read and accept the terms and conditions.
Official check for completeness, correctness and authorization of the signatories
Feedback usually within 5 working days via e-mail and ePortal
eportal_onboardingTR_overview_cardText_f: Uploading a digitally signed declaration of intent for identity verification
e-ID verification for individuals and sole proprietorships
eportal_onboardingTR_overview_cardText_s: Select the Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) with which you identify yourself to end users in the swiyu ecosystem and verify your identity.
eportal_onboardingTR_overview_cardText_t: Technical proof of ownership using the API and DID toolbox
eportal_onboardingTR_overview_inroText_T: An entry in the trust registry strengthens your position in the swiyu ecosystem. When you interact with end users, you appear as officially verified and trustworthy. Verification is done via your Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs). These act as digital identifiers that uniquely and securely prove your identity. This applies, for example, when collecting data, issuing certificates, managing memberships, or sending tickets.
The fee already paid for the official examination is non-refundable in the event of a rejection.
Note: A regular application is subject to official review. In the test environment, your application is only checked automatically.
eportal_onboardingTR_selectDID_introText: Each Decentralized Identifier (DID) is used to uniquely identify applicants as a private individual, company or organization to end users. In order for the DIDs to appear as verified and trustworthy in the trust register, they must be verified beforehand.
eportal_onboardingTR_selectDID_pageTitle: Select DIDs for the Trust Register
eportal_onboardingTR_selectDID_sectionIn: Select one or more DIDs to be registered. A technical proof of ownership must be provided for each DID in the next step. DIDs that are not selected can also be checked at a later date.
eportal_onboardingTR_success_copy_TE: Thank you for updating your profile. Your application has been automatically reviewed and you have been successfully entered into the trust register.
eportal_onboardingTR_technicalProof_comp: Use the status display above to check whether your specialist has successfully submitted the technical proofs of possession. If all DIDs are displayed as verified, you can submit the application.
eportal_onboardingTR_technicalProof_hand: Use the e-mail template to share links to the documentation and this process step. Alternatively, your specialist can open the verification process directly from the administration overview and take over for you at this point.
eportal_onboardingTR_technicalProof_info: Entry in the trust register also requires technical proof that the applicant organization has control over the DIDs used. For this purpose, a proof of possession must be generated for each DID and transmitted via the API.
eportal_onboardingTR_technicalProof_prov: Have your DID strings, private keys and your access token for the API ready. Call the API to generate a unique identification code (nonce) for each DID.
eportal_onboardingTR_technicalProof_crea: A proof of possession is valid for 24 hours.
Create a proof of possession with the DID toolbox. It contains the DID string, the corresponding private key and the unique identification code.
eportal_onboardingTR_technicalProof_send: If not already done, your technical specialist must register in the ePortal. To do this, send an invitation code via the ePortal. This allows the person to access the swiyu application and your DID list.
eportal_onboardingTR_technicalProof_subm: Submit the proofs of possession via the API within 24 hours. All proofs must be submitted in the same request. You will need to authenticate again with your access token.
eportal_onboarding_development_adminOver: The above information can be accessed at any time in the administration overview. Simply click on the “Set up DID” button below your DID overview.
eportal_onboarding_development_emailTemp:
Hello

You are invited to complete a basic technical setup for the swiyu ecosystem.


-- Sign in to ePortal: Use an eIAM-compatible login to sign in to ePortal.

ePortal: https://eportal.admin.ch/


-- Access authorization: You need an invitation code. This code can be generated by the person sending this e-mail.

Generate code: https://swiyu-admin-ch.github.io/cookbooks/onboarding-base-and-trust-registry/#5-user-management

Redeem code: https://eportal.admin.ch/redeem


-- Setup steps: Follow the instructions in the swiyu Service Portal to create an initial identifier. Prompts, a toolbox, and additional technical documentation are available.

Direct link: {handoverUrl}

swiyu Service Portal: {servicePortalUrl}

Open manually: Navigate to the swiyu Service Portal. Open the organization or business partner from the list. Click "Set up DID" on the following page to display the setup steps.


Thank you and kind regards
Handover of technical setup for the swiyu ecosystem
eportal_onboarding_development_handover_: Share the link to the technical documentation with your specialist. It contains complete instructions, covering everything from the swiyu APIs to the use of the DID Toolbox.
eportal_onboarding_development_introText: To interact with customers in the swiyu ecosystem, a decentralized identifier, a so‑called Decentralized Identifier (DID), is first created for you.
eportal_onboarding_development_invitatio: Invite a specialist responsible for development. This person must first register in the ePortal. With the code, they can then access the swiyu application on behalf of your organization.
eportal_onboarding_development_preparati: Have your access tokens, API URL and business partner ID ready. With them you can call the API via the terminal to obtain the DID space (Identifier Registry ID) and a corresponding URL (Identifier Registry URL). These and the toolbox are required for the next step. The DID Toolbox, including documentation, is provided on GitHub.
eportal_onboarding_development_sectionTi: How would you like to proceed?
Guidance for handing over the setup
eportal_onboarding_development_setup_car: Assign your specialist to set up a DID for you. All the necessary information, including links, can be found via the “Set up DID” button in the administration overview.
eportal_onboarding_development_swiyuAPI_: Save the access tokens. They are only displayed once.
Register for the following APIs. This is required at least once but can be repeated for each project.
eportal_onboarding_development_toolbox_c: The toolbox can locally store a key pair for you. Alternatively, you can use self‑generated keys.
Run the toolbox and create a DID

Follow the instructions to create a DID log using the DID Toolbox. This can be stored in your DID space via the API. Once your first DID has been successfully set up, it will be displayed in the admin overview.
eportal_onboarding_didInstruction_introT: To interact with end users in the swiyu ecosystem, at least one DID (Decentralized Identifier) must be created. You use this identifier to identify yourself to others. When developing multiple digital offerings, it can be useful to create a separate DID for each service.
eportal_onboarding_error_technicalProof: Please send the proofs of ownership via API
eportal_onboarding_error_trustLevel: Please select if you want to purchase a profile verification
eportal_onboarding_finalisation_introTex: You can now proceed with setting up the DIDs and integrating them with your digital services.
eportal_onboarding_payment_info: Your payment has been successfully processed. Thank you.
eportal_onboarding_product_cardFeature_1: Access to the swiyu trust infrastructure
eportal_onboarding_product_cardFeature_2: Quick start as a non-verified organization
eportal_onboarding_product_cardFeature_3: Create IDs and request user data
eportal_onboarding_product_cardFeature_6: Verified identity when interacting with end users
Preview of verification via swiyu Wallet
eportal_onboarding_product_cardSubline_B: You can adjust your trust level later
You need a Swiss UID or e-ID for verification
eportal_onboarding_product_cardSubline_T: Configuration cannot be transferred to the production environment.
eportal_onboarding_product_card_cardSubt: Test services and integrations without commitment
eportal_onboarding_product_gov_slider: View prices for public administration and government agencies
eportal_onboarding_product_infrastructur: Use the public swiyu ecosystem productively for your use cases – for example, for issuing employee ID cards or integrating an identity check into your e-commerce process.
eportal_onboarding_product_introText: The swiyu trust infrastructure is the secure foundation for decentralized digital identities (Self-Sovereign Identity) in Switzerland, such as the e-ID. It can be used for commercial, private or charitable purposes.
eportal_onboarding_product_playground_ca: Develop and validate your business scenarios without risk. This environment provides test certificates and test tokens that cannot be used productively.
eportal_onboarding_profile_actorType_inf: Select the profile type that corresponds to your organization or the intended use. Government agencies include cantons, municipalities, and federal offices.
eportal_onboarding_profile_confirmation: I confirm that all details provided above are correct and up to date. *
eportal_onboarding_profile_contact_infoT: A group email address or a central phone number is sufficient, provided that someone familiar with your swiyu registration can be reached. Fields marked with an asterisk * are required.
What contact information should I provide?
eportal_onboarding_profile_error_inputLa: Please enter the street name and house number
eportal_onboarding_profile_info: No proof of identity is required to participate in the swiyu ecosystem. In order to finally be displayed as verified and trustworthy, you must trigger a manual check of your details after the basic setup. You will need an e-ID or UID for this.
eportal_onboarding_profile_infoText_orga: Organisation details are required for registration.
No real organisation details are required for registration in the test environment and its use. However, we recommend entering a name that clearly indicates the test environment.
eportal_onboarding_profile_inputLabel_na: Please enter the name of the organization
This name will be visible to others and will replace your current profile name.
eportal_onboarding_profile_inputLabel_po: Please enter the number in the correct format: 1234
eportal_onboarding_profile_inputLabel_ui: Please enter your Swiss company identification number
Please enter your UID in the correct format: CHE-111.222.333
eportal_onboarding_profile_introText: Whenever your digital services interact with end users, it becomes visible who you are. If you want to appear as a trustworthy organisation in the swiyu ecosystem, you can have the details below officially verified later.
eportal_onboarding_profile_language_info: The name you choose first serves as the default name displayed to users. For example, if only a default name in German is defined, it will also be displayed on French-language devices. However, if your profile name has official translations, you can add them individually for each language.
eportal_onboarding_profile_organisation_: Your information should match your entry in the commercial register so that it can be verified at a later date. Otherwise, we recommend using the information from your incorporation documents.
What information should I provide here?
eportal_onboarding_profile_uid_infoText: The UID is an identification number for organizations in Switzerland. It is assigned, for example, to companies listed in the Commercial Register or to public institutions. Identification numbers from other countries are not permitted and will be rejected during verification.
eportal_onboarding_profile_uid_infoTitle: What does the UID stand for?
eportal_onboarding_start_cardText_handov: To become part of the swiyu ecosystem, technical setup is required. A toolbox and developer documentation are available for this purpose.
eportal_onboarding_start_cardText_paymen: For productive use of the swiyu trust infrastructure, selecting a commercial option is required. Complete the purchase by choosing a payment option.
eportal_onboarding_start_cardText_produc: Start with infrastructure access for productive use or explore the technical features in the test environment. Then define the trust level that suits your organization.
eportal_onboarding_start_cardText_profil: Create a swiyu profile as a company, authority, governmental or non-governmental organization, association, or individual. No verification is required in this step.
eportal_onboarding_start_introText: The swiyu trust infrastructure forms the backbone of the Swiss e-ID. It is open to all people and organizations and can be integrated into digital services.
eportal_onboarding_start_pageTitle: Welcome to the swiyu Service Portal
eportal_onboarding_start_sectionText_swi: With the e-ID, Swiss citizens and people living in Switzerland can verify their identity online via the swiyu wallet. The trust infrastructure also supports the issuance of credentials by other bodies.
eportal_onboarding_start_sectionTitle_re: Registration process for the swiyu ecosystem
eportal_onboarding_start_sectionTitle_sw: Learn more about the swiyu ecosystem and the e-ID
eportal_site_onboarding_trust_introducti: Technischer Besitznachweis wird automatisiert auf Aktualität und Konsistenz überprüft
eportal_verifyAdditionalDIDs_createProof: A proof of possession is valid for 24 hours.
A proof of possession must be created with the DID Toolbox for both the new DIDs and the verified one. It contains the DID string, the corresponding private key and the one-time identification code.
eportal_verifyAdditionalDIDs_introText: If you want to use new DIDs to identify yourself as a verified organisation with your digital offering, a technical proof of possession must be provided. There is a simplified process for this, in which the proof can be submitted using an already verified DID.
eportal_verifyAdditionalDIDs_prepareData: Have the new DIDs, any already verified DID, the private keys and your access token for the API ready. Call the API to generate a one-time identification code (nonce) for each DID.
eportal_verifyAdditionalDIDs_submitProof: Submit the generated proofs via the API within 24 hours. Authenticate again with your access token. All proofs must be submitted in the same API request, as the already verified DID is used to verify the new DIDs. The rest is automated and the new status of the DIDs will be confirmed in the administration overview.
